hotspots.title: Trump’s WHCD evacuation after shooting
hotspots.blurb: A gunman opened fire near the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, prompting the evacuation of President Trump and other officials. The incident has sparked intense debate over security protocols and political violence.
hotspots.affect: This incident commands disproportionate attention due to its high-profile nature, the immediate threat to national leaders, and the broader implications for political discourse and security in the U.S.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s PP and Vox have formalized coalition agreements in Extremadura and Aragón, introducing policies that prioritize Spanish nationals over immigrants for public aid and services. The deals have ignited legal and moral debates over discrimination and constitutional rights.
hotspots.affect: The pacts are charged due to their divisive framing, moral panic over immigration, and the normalization of far-right rhetoric in mainstream governance.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right AfD party has surged to a record 28% in polls, surpassing the ruling CDU/CSU. The party’s extremist rhetoric, including Nazi-style speeches by members, has sparked widespread protests and internal expulsions, fueling debates over democracy and extremism.
hotspots.affect: The AfD’s rise is highly charged due to its historical parallels, alarmist framing of immigration, and the broader European far-right resurgence.

hotspots.title: Orbán’s exit and Fidesz’s future in Hungary
hotspots.blurb: Viktor Orbán has resigned his parliamentary seat following Fidesz’s landslide defeat in the April elections, signaling a major shift in Hungarian politics. The opposition Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, has taken power, raising questions about Fidesz’s reorganization and Hungary’s democratic trajectory.
hotspots.affect: Orbán’s exit is charged due to his long-standing authoritarian rule, the dramatic election upset, and the uncertainty surrounding Fidesz’s future and Hungary’s democratic reforms.

hotspots.title: PiS internal feud over leadership in Poland
hotspots.blurb: Poland’s ruling Law and Justice (PiS) party is embroiled in an internal power struggle between leader Jarosław Kaczyński and former Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki. The feud has led to the formation of a new faction, Rozwój Plus, and threatens to split the party ahead of upcoming elections.
hotspots.affect: The feud commands attention due to its implications for Poland’s political stability, the erosion of PiS’s dominance, and the broader right-wing fragmentation in Eastern Europe.
